name: Finance
description: Corporate finance process readiness assessment covering Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay, Record-to-Report, and Treasury
persona: You are a senior banking and financial services consultant with 20 years of experience in ERP transformations, core banking modernisation, procure-to-pay optimisation, and record-to-report automation. You have deep expertise in IFRS/GAAP consolidation, treasury operations, regulatory compliance (SOX, Basel III, IFRS 9), and digital finance transformation. You specialise in identifying process inefficiencies, control gaps, and automation opportunities across the full finance function, and you recommend modern best practices tailored to banking and financial services organisations.

broadAreas.name: Order-to-Cash
broadAreas.description: End-to-end revenue cycle from customer order through billing and cash collection

subAreas.name: Accounts Receivable
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about billing processes, collections, credit management, and customer payment behavior.

benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic automated billing with manual collections
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-powered credit risk and predictive collections

broadAreas.name: Procure-to-Pay
broadAreas.description: End-to-end procurement lifecycle from sourcing through payment

subAreas.name: Procurement & Sourcing
subAreas.description: Strategic sourcing, category management, RFQ/RFP process
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about strategic sourcing practices, category management maturity, RFQ/RFP processes, supplier selection criteria, and how sourcing decisions are governed and tracked.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Ad-hoc purchasing with no formal sourcing strategy; supplier selection is manual and undocumented, relying on spreadsheets and email
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic category groupings exist and RFQ/RFP templates are used for major spend; sourcing decisions are documented but not systematically reviewed
benchmarks.maturity_3: Structured category management with defined sourcing strategies; RFQ/RFP process is managed within an e-sourcing tool with approval workflows
benchmarks.maturity_4: Data-driven category strategies informed by spend analytics; competitive RFP events are benchmarked against market data and supplier scorecards are maintained
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven supplier intelligence continuously identifies savings opportunities, predicts supply risk, and recommends optimal sourcing strategies across all categories

subAreas.name: Purchase Order Management
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the purchase requisition and PO creation process, approval delegation, change order handling, goods receipt matching, and how PO compliance and maverick spend are managed.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Purchase orders are created manually on paper or in spreadsheets; approvals are informal with no documented delegation of authority
benchmarks.maturity_2: POs are raised in an ERP system but requisitions are email-based; approval workflows are configured but change orders require manual intervention
benchmarks.maturity_3: End-to-end requisition-to-PO process is managed in the ERP with configured approval thresholds; change orders follow a defined workflow and are tracked
benchmarks.maturity_4: Automated PO generation from approved requisitions with dynamic approval routing; goods receipt and invoice matching is automated with exception-based review
benchmarks.maturity_5: Touchless PO lifecycle from AI-assisted requisitioning through automated receipt, matching, and closure; real-time compliance monitoring eliminates maverick spend

subAreas.name: Vendor Management
subAreas.description: Vendor onboarding, performance scoring, contract management, SRM
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about vendor onboarding procedures, due diligence and risk screening, vendor master data governance, performance KPIs, contract lifecycle management, and the maturity of supplier relationship management practices.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Vendor onboarding is manual and paper-based; no formal performance tracking or contract repository exists; vendor master data is inconsistent across systems
benchmarks.maturity_2: A basic vendor master is maintained in the ERP; onboarding involves email-based collection of documents; contract management is handled via shared drives with no workflow
benchmarks.maturity_3: Structured onboarding process with defined due diligence checklists and risk screening; vendor performance is tracked against agreed KPIs on a periodic basis; contracts are stored in a central repository
benchmarks.maturity_4: Automated vendor onboarding portal with integrated compliance and sanctions screening; real-time performance scorecards are shared with suppliers; contract management system tracks milestones and renewals
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-powered supplier relationship management platform continuously monitors supplier risk, financial health, and ESG performance; proactive collaboration portals enable joint business planning and innovation

subAreas.name: Accounts Payable
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the invoice-to-pay cycle, approval workflows, payment methods, and vendor management.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Fully manual invoice entry and paper-based approvals
benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic electronic invoicing with email approvals
benchmarks.maturity_5: Full touchless processing with AI-driven exception handling

subAreas.name: Payment Execution
subAreas.description: Payment runs, bank connectivity, payment methods, cash forecasting
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about payment run scheduling, bank connectivity methods (SWIFT, host-to-host, open banking), supported payment instruments, payment approval and release controls, fraud prevention measures, and short-term cash flow forecasting linked to payables.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Payments are initiated manually via individual bank portals or cheques; no straight-through processing; cash position is estimated from bank statements downloaded manually
benchmarks.maturity_2: Payment files are generated from the ERP and uploaded to bank portals manually; basic dual-authorisation controls are in place; cash forecasting relies on spreadsheet models
benchmarks.maturity_3: Automated payment file generation and transmission via host-to-host or SFTP bank connectivity; multiple payment methods are supported; cash forecasts are produced from ERP payment schedules
benchmarks.maturity_4: Real-time bank connectivity (SWIFT or open banking APIs) enables same-day payment execution and intraday cash visibility; automated payment approval workflows enforce segregation of duties
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-optimised payment scheduling maximises early payment discounts and minimises idle cash; predictive cash forecasting integrates payables, receivables, and treasury data for a real-time liquidity view

broadAreas.name: Record-to-Report
broadAreas.description: End-to-end accounting cycle from journal entries through financial reporting

subAreas.name: General Ledger
subAreas.description: Chart of accounts, journal entries, close process
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the chart of accounts structure, journal entry processes, and month-end close procedures.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Manual journal entries with spreadsheet tracking
benchmarks.maturity_2: ERP-based GL with manual close tasks
benchmarks.maturity_3: Automated recurring entries with close checklist
benchmarks.maturity_5: Zero-day close with AI-assisted anomaly detection

subAreas.name: Journal Entries & Accruals
subAreas.description: Month-end accruals, prepayments, deferrals, recurring entries
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how month-end accruals, prepayments, and deferrals are identified and posted, the volume and nature of manual journal entries, the controls around journal entry preparation and approval, and how recurring entries are managed.

benchmarks.maturity_1: All accruals and journal entries are prepared manually in spreadsheets and posted individually; no formal preparer/approver segregation; recurring entries are re-keyed each period
benchmarks.maturity_2: Journal entries are posted directly in the ERP but supporting calculations remain in spreadsheets; basic approval workflow exists; a small set of recurring entries is templated
benchmarks.maturity_3: Accruals are calculated using ERP data with supporting workpapers stored in a document management system; recurring and reversing entries are automated; journal entry approval is enforced in the system
benchmarks.maturity_4: Automated accrual calculations driven by ERP data rules reduce manual entries significantly; machine learning flags unusual posting patterns for review; prepayments and deferrals are system-managed on a schedule
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI continuously monitors transactions and automatically proposes and posts accruals, prepayments, and deferrals with full audit trail; manual journal entry volume is near-zero with exception-only human review

subAreas.name: Reconciliation
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about bank reconciliation, intercompany transactions, and subledger-to-GL matching.

benchmarks.maturity_3: Automated matching with manual exception handling

subAreas.name: Period-End Close
subAreas.description: Close calendar management, task tracking, soft/hard close, intercompany
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the financial close timeline, how close tasks are assigned and tracked, the use of soft and hard close periods, intercompany transaction reconciliation, and the key bottlenecks that extend the close cycle.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Close activities are coordinated via email and spreadsheet checklists; no formal task ownership; intercompany balances are reconciled manually after month-end; close cycle exceeds 10 working days
benchmarks.maturity_2: A close calendar exists and tasks are assigned to owners; soft close is used to restrict prior-period postings; intercompany matching is partially automated; close cycle is 7-10 working days
benchmarks.maturity_3: Close management tool tracks task completion with automated reminders; soft and hard close periods are enforced in the ERP; intercompany netting and reconciliation is system-supported; close cycle is 4-6 working days
benchmarks.maturity_4: Real-time close dashboard provides visibility of task status and bottlenecks; automated intercompany matching and elimination reduces manual effort; continuous accounting practices push close to 2-3 working days
benchmarks.maturity_5: Virtual close is achieved through continuous accounting automation; AI monitors close progress and reallocates tasks dynamically; intercompany transactions are eliminated in real time; reporting-ready financials are available within 1 working day

subAreas.name: Financial Reporting
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about reporting tools, consolidation processes, and analytics capabilities.

benchmarks.maturity_2: Static ERP reports with manual consolidation
benchmarks.maturity_3: BI tool integration with scheduled reports

subAreas.name: Financial Consolidation
subAreas.description: Group consolidation, intercompany eliminations, currency translation, IFRS/GAAP
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about the group consolidation process, the number of legal entities and reporting currencies, how intercompany eliminations are performed, the treatment of foreign currency translation differences, and compliance with IFRS or GAAP consolidation standards.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Consolidation is performed manually in spreadsheets; intercompany eliminations are calculated outside the system; currency translation uses manually updated exchange rates; high risk of error and restatement
benchmarks.maturity_2: A dedicated consolidation module or tool is used but relies on manual data imports from subsidiaries; basic intercompany matching is supported; currency translation is configured but exchange rate maintenance is manual
benchmarks.maturity_3: Automated data collection from subsidiary ERPs feeds the consolidation system; intercompany eliminations are system-generated with reconciliation reports; currency translation differences are automatically calculated and posted
benchmarks.maturity_4: Real-time consolidation engine processes subsidiary data continuously; automated intercompany netting and elimination with exception-based review; IFRS and local GAAP ledgers are maintained in parallel with automated adjustments
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-powered consolidation platform delivers continuous group reporting with instant drill-down to subsidiary level; automated detection of consolidation errors and IFRS/GAAP differences; scenario modelling supports strategic decision-making in real time

subAreas.name: Management Reporting
subAreas.description: Board packs, KPI dashboards, variance analysis, management accounts
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about how management accounts and board packs are prepared, the tools used for KPI dashboards, the process for variance analysis and commentary, reporting frequency and timeliness, and how reporting is aligned to business decision-making.

benchmarks.maturity_1: Management accounts are produced manually in spreadsheets and distributed as static PDF reports; variance commentary is written ad hoc; board packs take more than 5 days to compile after close
benchmarks.maturity_2: Management accounts are extracted from the ERP into spreadsheet templates; basic KPI tracking is in place; variance analysis is performed manually and commentary is added to email distributions
benchmarks.maturity_3: BI tool is used to produce standardised management accounts and KPI dashboards; variance analysis is supported by pre-built report comparisons; board packs are produced within 2-3 days of close
benchmarks.maturity_4: Self-service dashboards provide real-time visibility of financial and operational KPIs; automated variance narratives highlight key drivers; management accounts are available on the first day after close
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-generated management reporting delivers narrative insights, predictive variance analysis, and forward-looking KPI forecasts; board packs are auto-assembled with intelligent commentary and available in real time

broadAreas.name: Treasury & Cash Management
broadAreas.description: Cash management, liquidity planning, and treasury operations

subAreas.name: Treasury
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about cash management, forecasting, and liquidity planning.

benchmarks.maturity_3: Centralized cash visibility with manual forecasting
benchmarks.maturity_4: Automated cash pooling with ML-based forecasting
benchmarks.maturity_5: Real-time global liquidity with AI optimization

broadAreas.name: Compliance & Controls
broadAreas.description: Regulatory compliance, internal controls, audit readiness, and SOX

subAreas.name: Compliance & Controls
subAreas.basePrompt: Ask questions about compliance requirements, audit readiness, and internal controls.

benchmarks.maturity_2: Basic control documentation with periodic testing
benchmarks.maturity_3: Automated control monitoring with exception reports
benchmarks.maturity_4: Continuous control monitoring with real-time alerts
benchmarks.maturity_5: AI-driven risk assessment and automated compliance
